title: Quiz: Matrices
description: Test your understanding of matrices and their role in quantum computing

questions.question: What is the result of multiplying a 2×3 matrix by a 3×2 matrix?
questions.explanation: For matrix multiplication A(m×n) × B(n×p), the result is m×p. So 2×3 times 3×2 gives 2×2.

questions.question: Which matrix property must ALL quantum gates satisfy?
questions.explanation: All quantum gates must be unitary to preserve normalization of quantum states. Hermitian matrices represent observables, not gates.

questions.question: What is the determinant of the Pauli-X matrix [[0,1],[1,0]]?
If A† = A (Hermitian) and U†U = I (unitary), which statement is true?

options.text: A matrix can be both Hermitian and unitary
No matrix can be both Hermitian and unitary

questions.explanation: A matrix can be both! For example, the Pauli matrices X, Y, Z are both Hermitian (X†=X) and unitary (X†X=I).

questions.question: Matrix multiplication is commutative: AB = BA for all matrices A and B.
questions.explanation: False! Matrix multiplication is NOT commutative. In quantum computing, this means the order of gates matters.

questions.question: A matrix with determinant zero cannot be inverted.
questions.explanation: True! det(A) = 0 means A is singular and has no inverse. The inverse formula requires dividing by the determinant.

questions.question: What is the conjugate transpose of [[1, i], [0, 2]]?
questions.explanation: Conjugate transpose: transpose then conjugate. Transpose gives [[1,0],[i,2]], then conjugate i→-i gives [[1,0],[-i,2]].

questions.question: Which of the following are properties of unitary matrices? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: a) ✓ Definition of unitary. b) ✓ |det(U)|=1 always for unitary. c) ✓ Key property - preserves norms and angles. d) ✗ Not required - H gate is unitary but not Hermitian.
